mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.65% as of 2017-01-15 indicates accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising monthly payroll employment (PAYEMS) above trend signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which increases corporate earnings expectations and drives positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth exceeds 150,000 persons for two consecutive months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity investors reprice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 472,142 million and HOUST at 1,226 thousand as of 2017-01-15
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between the joint signal and forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 3-month moving average signals supply-side weakness and reduced corporate capacity utilization, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors anticipate margin compression.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average by more than 0.5 index points
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following the trigger are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 1.5%) signals monetary tightening that increases real discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.3% AND FEDFUNDS > 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 244.158 and FEDFUNDS at 0.65% as of 2017-01-15
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of inflation and rates on forward returns is not statistically significant (interaction p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending power, which compresses corporate earnings and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.0% for two consecutive months
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) growth above 1,200 thousand units signals robust residential investment and forward consumer confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as construction and related sectors expand.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,226 thousand as of 2017-01-15 indicates sustained housing demand
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during periods of HOUST > 1,200 are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals robust aggregate demand and consumer spending momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as corporate revenue and profit expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5% AND GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth exceeds 2.0% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 472,142 million and GDPC1 at 16,804.8 billion as of 2017-01-15
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumption and investment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or the correlation between the joint signal and forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.12).
